en: It's so good to see you, Poe.
Have you lost weight? I can almost put my wings around you!
ko: 살 빠졌어? 날개로 거의 안을 수 있겠어!

en: Uh, well, maybe a little. Oh, poor you! You must feel weak!
ko: 어, 뭐, 아마 조금. 오, 불쌍해라! 힘이 빠졌겠구나!

en: Let me get you some soup. Ah, no, that's okay, Dad. I'm not hungry.
ko: 국 좀 가져다줄게. 아, 아니 괜찮아, 아빠. 배 안 고파.

en: Not hungry? Poe, are you alright?
ko: 배가 안 고파? 포, 괜찮은 거야?

en: Yeah, yeah, no, I'm fine. I just...
Earlier today, I was fighting these bandits. Nothing too dangerous. I mean, they were just, you know...
ko: 오늘 아까 산적이랑 싸웠거든. 별로 위험하진 않았어. 그러니까 걔네는 그냥, 있잖아...

en: Yeah? And then, the strangest thing happened.
ko: 그래? 그런데 아주 이상한 일이 일어났어.

en: Uh, I had this crazy vision. I think I saw my mom and me as a baby.
ko: 어, 이상한 환상을 봤어. 엄마랑 아기 때의 나를 본 것 같아.

en: What? A mom? A baby?
Um, Dad... How do I say this?
Where did I come from?
Well, you see, uh, son, baby geese come from a little egg.
ko: 그게 말이야, 어, 아들아, 아기 거위는 작은 알에서 나와.

en: Don't ask me where the egg comes from.
Dad, that's not what I meant. I know it's not.
ko: 아빠, 그런 뜻이 아니야. 그게 아닌 거 알아.

en: I think it's time I told you something I should have told you a long time ago.
ko: 오래전에 말했어야 할 걸 이제 말해야 할 때가 된 것 같아.

en: Okay.
You might have been kind of, uh...
adopted. I knew it!
You knew? Well, who told you? No one. I mean, come on, Dad.
ko: 알고 있었어? 누가 말해줬어? 아무도. 그러니까, 아빠.

en: But if you knew, why didn't you ever say anything?
ko: 근데 알았으면 왜 아무 말도 안 했어?

en: Why didn't you say anything?
How did I get here, Dad? Where did I come from?
ko: 여기 어떻게 온 거야, 아빠? 난 어디서 왔어?

en: Actually, you came from this.
It was just another day at the restaurant.
Time to make the noodles. I went out to the back,
where my vegetables had just been delivered.
There were cabbages, tuna, radishes. Only there were no radishes.
ko: 양배추, 참치, 무가 있었어. 근데 무가 없었어.

en: Just a very hungry baby panda.
There was no note. Of course, you could have eaten it.
ko: 쪽지는 없었어. 물론, 네가 먹었을 수도 있지.

en: I waited for someone to come looking for you.
But no one did.
No one. I brought you inside.
Fed you. Gave you a bath.
And fed you again. And again.
And tried to put some pants on you.
And then I made a decision that would change my life forever.
ko: 그리고 내 인생을 영원히 바꿀 결정을 내렸어.

en: To make my soup without radishes.
And to raise you as my own son.
My little panda. And from that moment on,
ko: 내 작은 팬더. 그리고 그 순간부터,

en: both my soup and my life have been that much sweeter.
ko: 내 국과 내 인생 모두 그만큼 더 달콤해졌어.

en: And little Po, that's the end of the story.
Look at me. No, don't look at me.
ko: 나 좀 봐. 아니, 보지 마.

en: That's it? That can't be it.
ko: 그게 다야? 그게 다일 리가 없어.

en: There's gotta be more, Dad. Well, there was the time you ate all my bamboo furniture.
ko: 더 있어야 해, 아빠. 글쎄, 네가 내 대나무 가구를 다 먹어치운 적이 있었지.

en: It was important, too. One dumpling, please.
Dragon warrior size.
Oh, Po, your story may not have such a happy beginning, but look how it turned out.
ko: 오, 포, 네 이야기의 시작이 행복하지 않았을 수 있어. 하지만 결과를 봐.

en: You got me, you got kung fu, and you got noodles.
ko: 나도 있고, 쿵푸도 있고, 국수도 있잖아.

en: I know, I just have so many questions.
Like, how did I ever fit in this tiny basket?
ko: 예를 들면, 이 작은 바구니에 어떻게 들어갔지?

en: Why didn't I like pants? And who am I?
ko: 왜 바지를 싫어했지? 그리고 나는 누구지?
